title: Murine CD8 T cell restimulation in vitro
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"9rimv","text":"This protocol outlines the steps for re-stimulating engineered murine CD8 T cells in vitro.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"bh4n3","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"elu4g","text":"In brief: Transduced T cells were re-stimulated with irradiated Thy1.2+ splenocytes pulsed with Msln404-414 peptide (GQKMNAQAI, 1ug/mL) and IL-2 (50 IU/ml) for 5 days following T cell activation with a-CD3e/CD28. Peptides were purchased from Elim Peptide at \u003e80% purity. Three days after the second transduction, \u003e40% (TCROTI) and \u003e85% (TCR1045) of T cells expressed the transduced TCR or construct. On day 7 after antigen re-stimulation, \u003e90% of T cells expressed the transduced TCR.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sub","offset":100,"length":7},{"style":"sub","offset":322,"length":3},{"style":"sub","offset":340,"length":4}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"d2kg2","text":"You will need access to an irradiator to prepare irradiated antigen-presenting cells for this protocol. Access to this equipment often requires a federal background check. Reach out to your institution to determine appropriate requirements to access this equipment.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"w8RQv","text":"MATERIALS","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":null,"entityRanges":null,"data":null},{"key":"QmYD5","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":null,"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":null},{"key":"21xil","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":null,"entityRanges":[{"key":1,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":null},{"key":"WMBLf","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":null,"entityRanges":[{"key":2,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":null}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"reagents","mutability":"","data":{"id":127822,"guid":"41A25C37341F11EE9D4302C0B41BC903","name":"Falcon 50 mL polypropylene conical tube","mol_weight":0,"sku":"352070","url":"","cas_number":"","rrid":"","vendor":{"id":40,"name":"Corning","link":"https://www.corning.com/worldwide/en.html","is_requested":false,"image":{"source":"/img/vendors/placeholder.png","placeholder":"/img/vendors/placeholder.png"}},"is_public":true,"can_edit":false}},"1":{"type":"reagents","mutability":"","data":{"id":127823,"guid":"41A25C89341F11EE9D4302C0B41BC903","name":"15 mL polypropylene centrifuge tube","mol_weight":0,"sku":"430791","url":"","cas_number":"","rrid":"","vendor":{"id":40,"name":"Corning","link":"https://www.corning.com/worldwide/en.html","is_requested":false,"image":{"source":"/img/vendors/placeholder.png","placeholder":"/img/vendors/placeholder.png"}},"is_public":true,"can_edit":false}},"2":{"type":"reagents","mutability":"","data":{"id":127827,"guid":"41A25DCC341F11EE9D4302C0B41BC903","name":"Falcon cell strainer, 70um, nylon","mol_weight":0,"sku":"352350","url":"","cas_number":"","rrid":"","vendor":{"id":40,"name":"Corning","link":"https://www.corning.com/worldwide/en.html","is_requested":false,"image":{"source":"/img/vendors/placeholder.png","placeholder":"/img/vendors/placeholder.png"}},"is_public":true,"can_edit":false}}}}
steps: 1. Obtain spleens to pulse with peptide. Record pertinent information regarding donor mice (mouse background, number of spleens, gender, date of birth). 2. Harvest T cells by smashing spleens through a 40um filter. Rinse with 10-20 mL of T cell media and transfer to a 15 or 50 mL tube. Spin down @369 xg for 4-5 min at 4C. 3. ACK Lysis with 2 ml/spleen for 2 min at room temperature. 4. Quench ACK lysis with T cell media (use an equal or greater amount than the ACK volume). 5. Spin down @369 xg for 4-5 min at 4C. Resuspend in T cell media and split cells into separate 15 mL tubes to irradiate (1 tube per peptide condition; aliquot 1ml of cells / tube.). 6. Irradiate at 3500 rad (35 Gy). 7. Rinse cells immediately by filling tube with 10-20 mL of T cell media. Spin down at @369 xg for 4-5 min at 4C. Resuspend in 1-2 mL T cell media. The volume will depend on the number of spleens/cells in the tube. The goal is to pulse with excess peptide. Typically 1ug of peptide is sufficient to pulse up to 5 spleens worth of cells. 8. Add appropriate amount of peptide per tube. (e.g. 1ug of peptide for 1-5 spleens) 9. Mix well and incubate at 37C for 60 to 90 minutes (can go longer if necessary). 10. Rinse excess peptide out by washing with 10-12 mL T cell media. 11. Rinse a second time with 10-12 mL T cell media. 12. Count APCs. 13. Spin down T cells @369 xg for 4-5 min at 4C. Remove supernatant and resuspend in 10 mL T cell media. Count T cells. 14. Combine APCs and T cells at a 10:1 ratio (you can also do up to a 1:1 ratio if you don’t have enough APCs, but we have found the 10:1 APC:T cell ratio yields the best T cell expansion). Add T cell media to bring to 10 mL final volume in T25 flasks. Add 50 IU/mL IL-2. Ideally, plate 1-2e6 T cells and 10-20e6 APCs in an upright T25 flask. For larger T cell preparations, scale up in larger cell culture flasks proportionally.

1. If the flasks are starting to change from orange to yellow in color, add a small volume of media to the flask (up to 10mL in a T25). Do not over-dilute the cells - this will slow their growth and can result in cell death. It if okay to let cells grow in orange media. 2. Add 50 IU/mL of IL-2.
